channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Commercial vessels and American warships are coming under Iranian attack around the Strait of Hormuz as a new U.S. maritime protection plan has kicked off. The post U.S. AH-64 Apache, MH-60 Seahawk Helicopters Sink Six Iranian Boats (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying | The War Zone
item.description: The first new tanker is set to arrive in Israel in around a month and will help relieve pressure on the IAF's tiny and very old KC-707 tanker force.. The post Israel’s First KC-46 Tanker Is Now Flying appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ How Hegseth, Caine, and Hurst defended their budget strategy on the Hill | Breaking Defense
item.description: Valerie Insinna has the highlights from last week’s House and Senate hearings on the budget.

item.title: ⚛️ With its third SSBN, India edges closer to continuous at-sea deterrence | ASPI Strategist
item.description: India’s commissioning of the submarine INS Aridhaman last month is significant as a strategic marker in the evolution of India’s sea-based nuclear deterrent. In adding a third boat to India’s fleet of nuclear-powered ballistic missile ...

item.title: 🏛️ Anna Eshoo Joins Bipartisan Commission on Biodefense | Atlantic Council
item.description: WASHINGTON, DC – September 11, 2025 – The Bipartisan Commission on Biodefense is pleased to welcome former Congresswoman Anna Eshoo of California as a new Commissioner. Representative Eshoo served her constituents – including those in Silicon Valley – from 1993 until the end of…

item.title: 🏛️ Career submariner selected to perform duties of under secretary of the Navy | Defense News
item.description: Retired Navy Capt. William Toti is filling in as the Navy’s No. 2 civilian.

item.title: 🌍 First Ships Transit Strait Of Hormuz Under New U.S. Protection Plan | The War Zone
item.description: Project Freedom appears to be aimed, at least for now, at evacuating ships stuck in the Persian Gulf since the war with Iran began. The post First Ships Transit Strait Of Hormuz Under New U.S. Protection Plan appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Firewalls won’t protect GEOINT companies. Cyber resilience will, if we act now. | Breaking Defense
item.description: As cyberattacks increasingly aim to disrupt operations rather than just steal data, GEOINT providers need models that can continue operations under attack, Norman Laudermilch writes.

item.title: 🌍 US destroys six Iranian small boats, shoots down missiles, drones, admiral says | Defense News
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said Iran was trying to interfere with a U.S. military operation to open the Strait of Hormuz to commercial traffic.

item.title: 🏛️ Former Qatari aircraft on track for summer Air Force One delivery | Defense News
item.description: The new aircraft in the Boeing VC-25B Bridge program completed modifications and flight testing and is now being painting red, white and blue.

item.title: 🏴 Confusion Surrounds Future Of U.S. Long-Range Missiles In Germany Amid Rift With White House | The War Zone
item.description: The latest U.S.-Germany rift could leave NATO in Europe outmatched by Russia’s long-range firepower, at least in the near term. The post Confusion Surrounds Future Of U.S. Long-Range Missiles In Germany Amid Rift With White House appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Syria needs a trustworthy digital ecosystem to support its revival | Atlantic Council
item.description: Robust digital infrastructure, digital literacy, and cybersecurity are no longer luxuries for high-income nations but fundamental requirements for national stability and economic resilience. The post Syria needs a trustworthy digital ecosystem to support its revival appeared…

item.title: 📍 UAE to get 10 C-390 aircraft, up to 10 more in the future | Breaking Defense
item.description: “This also marks the aircraft’s first entry into the Middle East, reflecting global confidence in the efficiency of our defense system, while supporting the localization of military industries and strengthening the state’s industrial integration,” Vice President Sheikh Mansour…

item.title: 📍 From Consensus to Consequence: Rethinking ASEAN’s Myanmar Approach | The Diplomat
item.description: The self-admitted failure of ASEAN’s “Five-Point Consensus” demands an escalatory response.

item.title: ⚛️ Project Anthracite: Imagery Search for Possible Nuclear, Chemical, and Biological Defense Battalions | 38 North
item.description: The research in this article stems from Project Anthracite, a multiyear initiative funded by ... The post Project Anthracite: Imagery Search for Possible Nuclear, Chemical, and Biological Defense Battalions appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🏴 Will Any Central Asian Leaders Head to Moscow for Victory Day 2026? | The Diplomat
item.description: With the celebration expected to be toned down under possible threat of Ukrainian drone strikes, fewer foreign dignitaries are expected.

item.title: 🏛️ First B-52 To Arrive For Re-Engining At Boeing Plant Later This Year (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: New Rolls-Royce F130 engines are a critical upgrade for Air Force B-52s, but work has been beset by delays and growing costs. The post First B-52 To Arrive For Re-Engining At Boeing Plant Later This Year (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. War in Iran Leaves Ukraine’s Air Defense in Limbo | Foreign Policy
item.description: Ukraine and its partners are holding their breath to see what’s next for Patriot air defense missile deliveries.

item.title: 📍 ‘Millions of drones’: What to expect from Turkey’s SAHA 2026 defense expo | Breaking Defense
item.description: “SAHA has evolved from a national showcase into a genuine global meeting point for the defense and aerospace industry, and the 2026 edition will reflect this maturity,” one expert said.

item.title: 🏴 Looking at Europe With a Sharper Eye | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 📍 Mali Is the Key to Understanding Africa’s Trajectory | Foreign Policy
item.description: The West ignores the warning signs in the Sahel at its own peril.

item.title: 📍 It’s a wrap: Space-BACN satellite laser link program shifts from DARPA to DIU | Breaking Defense
item.description: Space-BACN’s optical inter-satellite links and related tech are transitioning to the Defense Innovation Unit for a future on-orbit pathfinder, according to industry execs.

item.title: 🏛️ US clears $8.6 billion arms sales to Middle East countries, ‘waiving’ congressional review | Breaking Defense
item.description: The possible deals include three countries buying APKWS, and Qatar could be purchasing more Patriot interceptors.

item.title: 📍 When Rumors of a Drinking Problem Sunk a Cabinet Nomination | Foreign Policy
item.description: The so-called Tower fiasco took place in a very different Washington to today.

item.title: 🌏 What Orban’s 16-Year China Experiment Reveals About Europe | The Diplomat
item.description: Orban amplified and politicized Hungary’s China ties, but the deeper drivers behind them extend well beyond one illiberal leader.

item.title: 🔐 Chinese electric vehicle exports rise amid the oil crisis, posing a dilemma for importing countries | Atlantic Council
item.description: China is exporting a large number of electric vehicles around the world amid today's oil shock. The cars' internet connectivity, however, could make importers vulnerable to cyberattacks. The post Chinese electric vehicle exports rise amid the oil crisis, posing a dilemma for…

item.title: 🌏 The Contradictions Shaping Japan’s Russia Policy | The Diplomat
item.description: Will Japan’s Russia policy shift again – or settle into ambiguity?

item.title: 🛡️ Uzbekistan Aims to Expand Extremism Law and Grow List of Extremist Crimes | The Diplomat
item.description: In an attempt to further control religious expression, Uzbekistan has updated its blacklist of extremist sites and drafted a law to expand the list of related crimes.

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: Breaking the Architecture of Iran’s Regime Power | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: Breaking the Architecture of Iran’s Regime Power appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 International Crimes and Human Rights Violations Against Muslims in BJP-Ruled Indian States Require Urgent Action | Just Security
item.description: The international community must redouble efforts to press Indian authorities for accountability in growing violence and rights abuses against Muslims. The post International Crimes and Human Rights Violations Against Muslims in BJP-Ruled Indian States Require Urgent Action…
